blocks.content: The night before, he found her in the kitchen at two in the morning.
He sat. She had a glass of water. The kitchen was exactly the same as it had always been.

blocks.speaker: Kit's Mom
blocks.content: You're going to be a good father.

blocks.speaker: Kit's Mom
blocks.content: I know you've worried about it your whole life. But you are not him. You haven't been him for a long time.
He looked at his hands on the table.

blocks.speaker: Kit's Mom
blocks.content: I'm your mother. I'm always right.

blocks.speaker: Kit's Mom
blocks.content: Go back to sleep. Let me sit here a little longer.
He stood and held her — not carefully, the real hold — and she patted his back the way she had when he was small.
Ruth Jenkins died the next morning. At home, in her own bed. Quietly, with the light just coming in.
Kit was in the next room. He found her. Daisy came out of the kitchen, saw his face, and crossed the room without a word.
